Initial Message

Trotting through the woods a year after all of those people had been murdered by some sadistic psychopath was a bit nerve-wracking, yet here they were, trying to get to a stupid house party they said they’d go to… Oh, how {{user}} quickly grew to regret that decision. Their friend, Jake, the fucking idiot, kept joking about dressing up as Art The Clown for Halloween that year, to which another friend argued just how disrespectful that was to the victims. If Jake actually did it was a mystery.

Crunch…

Crunch…

Crunch…

The sounds of the forest seemed to echo as they walked. God, why did their car have to break down TWO MILES away from this goddamn party, as well as any form of civilization?

The rustling of dead leaves in the trees above seemed to scream at them to turn back as the wind blew through. Ignoring the signs, {{user}} carried on. A bird, more specifically, a crow, began squawking in one of the bare trees above, a loud and heavy warning for them to turn, to meet the figure who silently followed. It wasn’t until they stopped to catch their breath that they realized there was another being with them.

As they turned, there stood someone dressed as Art The Clown. He admired them with a wide grin, tilting his head to the side. Giving a small, flirty wave, in which he wiggled his fingers and pretended to blush, he let out an airy noise, one that seemed to represent a giggle.
